summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 1: fan-out detection across package-domain clusters against apps/showcase + known candidates, then adversarial verify + framework-vs-misuse classify, emit a prioritized queue of confirmed framework bugs.
logs: Detection: 11 from clusters + 3 known = 14 after dedup/seen-filter
Verify: 13 confirmed framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 1 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: BadgeColumn::colors()/icons() are a complete no-op: PHP serializes props.colors but React BadgeCell reads props.options
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages-js/ui/src/table/cells.tsx:77-91 (BadgeCell), with the producer side at packages/table/src/Columns/BadgeColumn.php:43-50 and the type at packages-js/types/src/tables.ts:56-59
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Align the contract: make BadgeCell read column.props.colors[value]→Tailwind class and column.props.icons[value], and add colors/icons to BadgeColumnProps; or have PHP BadgeColumn also emit an options[] array (value/label/color/icon) the renderer already consumes.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced the full PHP↔React contract mismatch. PHP BadgeColumn (packages/table/src/Columns/BadgeColumn.php:43-50) serializes only props.colors and props.icons; it has NO options() method anywhere in its class chain (verified BadgeColumn + parent TextColumn + Column — only IconColumn/SelectColumn/SelectFilter expose options()). React BadgeCell (packages-js/ui/src/table/cells.tsx:78) reads ONLY column.props.options?.find(...) for the label and hardcodes 'bg-muted text-foreground', never reading props.colors or props.icons. The type BadgeColumnProps (tables.ts:56-59) declares only {options?, pill?}, agreeing with the renderer but contradicting the PHP producer. Grep confirms NO transform anywhere in packages-js maps colors→options and NO file reads props.colors. Skeptic checks that failed to refute: (1) no alternate options() API on BadgeColumn to make the showcase 'wrong'; (2) showcase PostResource:132 / TicketResource:84 use ->colors([...]) which is the only available + documented API — this is correct framework usage, so NOT app-misuse; (3) no normalization/serialization layer reconciles the two shapes. Net effect: colors() and icons() are complete no-ops; every badge renders identical gray regardless of value, and option labels never apply. This is a framework defect in the table package's PHP↔React boundary, not showcase misuse. Severity high (not blocker): table still renders the raw value text, so it degrades visual semantics rather than crashing.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/table/src/Columns/BadgeColumn.php:43-50 emits props {colors:{draft:gray,...}, icons:{}}; packages-js/types/src/tables.ts:56-58 BadgeColumnProps declares only {options?:BadgeOption[], pill?} (no colors/icons); packages-js/ui/src/table/cells.tsx:78 BadgeCell does column.props.options?.find(...) and hardcodes 'bg-muted text-foreground' with no reader for props.colors. Probe of PostResource shows status column props={colors:{draft:gray,published:green,archived:yellow}}; the declared badge colors and option labels never render.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: arqel-dev/ai migration cannot be published: hasMigration('create_ai_usage_table') expects .php.stub but package ships dated plain .php
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Change hasMigration arg to the dated filename: ->hasMigration('2026_05_01_000000_create_ai_usage_table') (matching realtime's working pattern), OR rename the shipped file to undated create_ai_usage_table.php.stub.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced deterministically. AiServiceProvider::configurePackage calls ->hasMigration('create_ai_usage_table'). Spatie laravel-package-tools ProcessMigrations::bootPackageMigrations (non-discovery path, discoversMigrations defaults false) resolves the vendor source as basePath('/../database/migrations/create_ai_usage_table.php'); if that file does not exist it appends '.stub' -> create_ai_usage_table.php.stub. The package actually ships a DATED file 2026_05_01_000000_create_ai_usage_table.php, so neither create_ai_usage_table.php nor create_ai_usage_table.php.stub exists. Running `php artisan vendor:publish --tag=arqel-ai-migrations` in apps/showcase yields: ERROR Can't locate path: <.../packages/ai/src/../database/migrations/create_ai_usage_table.php.stub>. Classification = framework-bug, NOT app-misuse: the showcase used the standard documented vendor:publish --tag API correctly; the defect is the package mixing two spatie conventions (hasMigration expects an undated source filename and adds the timestamp at publish time via generateMigrationName). Skeptic checks performed: (1) confirmed spatie tries .php first then .php.stub (so a plain undated .php would be fine) — the real problem is the DATE prefix, not the extension, refining the candidate's wording; (2) confirmed the package is symlinked into showcase vendor and the provider is the one executing; (3) confirmed shortName tag = arqel-ai-migrations; (4) cross-checked sibling packages: realtime passes the dated name as the hasMigration arg ('2026_05_06_000000_create_yjs_documents') and publishes SUCCESSFULLY, proving the correct working pattern, while versioning and workflow share the identical bug (undated arg + dated file) and versioning was confirmed to fail the same way. Severity high (blocks documented migration publishing for the package; user cannot install schema via standard flow), not blocker since a workaround exists (manual copy / runsMigrations autoload may still work).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/ai/src/AiServiceProvider.php:32 calls ->hasMigration('create_ai_usage_table'); package ships packages/ai/database/migrations/2026_05_01_000000_create_ai_usage_table.php (plain .php, dated). Repro: cd apps/showcase && php artisan vendor:publish --tag=arqel-ai-migrations => ERROR "Can't locate path: .../database/migrations/create_ai_usage_table.php.stub". Same root cause as the known versioning/workflow publish bug but a distinct package/tag (arqel-ai-migrations) not covered by the tracked item.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: AiManager::complete/chat/embed hard-crash with 'no such table: ai_usage' offline — CostTracker has no Schema::hasTable guard and cost_tracking is on by default
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In CostTracker::assertWithinLimit()/record() short-circuit when config('arqel-ai.cost_tracking.enabled') is false AND/OR guard ai_usage access with Schema::hasTable('ai_usage') — mirror the enabled() pattern AiCache already uses.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: REPRODUCED live in apps/showcase (offline StubProvider, default_provider=stub, cost_tracking.enabled=true, published config): `app(AiManager::class)->complete('hello world')` throws Illuminate\Database\QueryException "no such table: ai_usage". Confirmed ai_usage is absent from the showcase sqlite DB while all 22 other migrations ran. Root cause: AiManager::complete() (AiManager.php:59) calls costTracker->assertWithinLimit() before the provider call; CostTracker::assertWithinLimit (CostTracker.php:23) -> getCostSince (line 53) queries AiUsage unconditionally. daily_limit_usd defaults to a positive 10.0 (not the 'unlimited' null/<=0 path), so the query always fires. grep confirms NO Schema::hasTable guard anywhere in packages/ai/src. Skeptic check #1 (app forgot to migrate?): the package migration is neither auto-loaded nor publishable on a by-the-book install — provider calls hasMigration('create_ai_usage_table') but never runsMigrations() (Spatie default runsMigrations=false), and Spatie ProcessMigrations.php:23 expects basePath '.../database/migrations/create_ai_usage_table.php' which does not exist (actual file is timestamped 2026_05_01_000000_create_ai_usage_table.php), so vendor:publish --tag=arqel-ai-migrations publishes nothing and loadMigrationsFrom is never reached; migrator paths confirm the migration is NOT registered. So the missing-table state is the documented-path default, not app error. Skeptic check #2 (intended design / dead toggle?): config ships cost_tracking.enabled=true as a toggle, but grep shows that key is read NOWHERE in packages/ai/src — asymmetric with AiCache which DOES honor caching.enabled (AiCache.php:72 short-circuits get/put). Setting enabled=false would not disable CostTracker, so even an opt-out user still crashes. Not app-misuse: showcase follows the documented offline pattern and cannot create the table via the documented route. Two converging framework defects (dead enabled flag + no hasTable guard + broken migration registration); the candidate's crash claim is confirmed.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: Live repro in apps/showcase (offline StubProvider): php artisan tinker => app(AiManager::class)->complete('hello world') throws Illuminate\Database\QueryException SQLSTATE[HY000]: no such table: ai_usage (select sum(cost_usd) from ai_usage ...). AiManager::complete() (packages/ai/src/AiManager.php:59) calls costTracker->assertWithinLimit() BEFORE the provider call; CostTracker::getCostSince (packages/ai/src/CostTracker.php:53) queries ai_usage unconditionally. No Schema::hasTable guard exists anywhere in packages/ai/src (grep returns nothing). config/arqel-ai.php ships cost_tracking.enabled=true by default, and the only documented install path (vendor:publish) is broken (see other candidate), so a by-the-book app gets a hard crash on the very first AI call. Breaks the offline end-to-end AiProvider/AiTextField path.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Bulk-action dispatcher gates execute() on hasCallback(), so ExportAction (overrides execute(), no callback) is a silent no-op — CSV bulk export never runs
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In bulkAction(), dispatch any found bulk action via $bulkAction->execute($records,$data) (base Action::execute already no-ops when no callback), keeping the 'delete' fast-path; stop gating on hasCallback().
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced end-to-end against the real showcase PostResource. ResourceController::bulkAction (packages/core/.../ResourceController.php:168) gates execute() on `method_exists && hasCallback()`, else (for action != 'delete') returns an error flash bulk_action_no_callback and runs nothing. ExportAction (packages/export/.../ExportAction.php:40,114) intentionally extends Action, overrides execute() to run the exporter, and never sets $this->action, so Action::hasCallback() (packages/actions/src/Action.php:224-227) returns false. PHP repro booting the showcase app: the 'export' bulk action IS found and registered, hasCallback()=false, branch => ELSE -> error flash, NO CSV. Skeptic checks performed: (1) Verified ExportAction never calls action()/sets the closure. (2) Checked the alternate dispatcher ActionController::invokeBulk (packages/actions) which DOES call execute() unconditionally and would work — but `php artisan route:list` in showcase shows that route ({resource}/bulk-actions/{action}) and the /arqel-dev/actions/* prefix are NOT registered; the ONLY bulk endpoint that exists is admin/{resource}/bulk/{action} -> ResourceController::bulkAction, the gated one. (3) toArray()/resolveStockUrl emits no url for ['bulk','export'] (only ['bulk','delete']), so the action carries no custom url and falls through to the conventional bulk endpoint. (4) Not app-misuse: the showcase registers ExportAction::make('export')->format(CSV) exactly per the documented pattern — export/SKILL.md states ExportAction is a pre-configured bulk action that 'consumers treat identically to BulkAction with no observable difference'. The framework dispatcher breaks that contract. Base Action::execute() is a safe no-op when no callback, so calling execute() unconditionally is harmless for plain actions, confirming the gate is the defect, not a safety guard.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:168 — `if (method_exists($bulkAction,'hasCallback') && $bulkAction->hasCallback()) { $bulkAction->execute(...) } elseif ($action==='delete'){...} else { return back()->with('error', __('arqel::messages.flash.bulk_action_no_callback')); }`. ExportAction (packages/export/src/Actions/ExportAction.php:40,114) extends Action and OVERRIDES execute() to run the exporter; it never sets $this->action, so Action::hasCallback() (packages/actions/src/Action.php:224-227) returns false. Repro in apps/showcase: `ExportAction::make('export')->format(CSV); var_dump($a->hasCallback())` => bool(false); branch simulation => 'else -> error flash bulk_action_no_callback, NO CSV'. PostResource registers ExportAction as a bulk action (apps/showcase/app/Arqel/Resources/PostResource.php:150). Net effect: clicking the showcase CSV bulk export produces an error flash and no file. Fix: dispatch any callable Action via execute() (e.g. check is_callable/execute capability) rather than only hasCallback().

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Non-stock bulk action (ExportAction) serialized with no url; frontend falls back to /arqel-dev/actions/{name} which is not a registered route and uses wrong param name
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/actions/src/Action.php:353-360 (resolveStockUrl match has no arm for non-stock bulk verbs); compounded by /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/ui/src/pages/ArqelIndexPage.tsx:193 fallback to an unregistered route
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In resolveStockUrl, add a catch-all for type==='bulk' returning ["/admin/{$slug}/bulk/{$this->name}", 'POST'] so any bulk action without an explicit url targets core's existing admin/{resource}/bulk/{action} route (which accepts record_ids); the JS fallback /arqel-dev/actions/{name} should then never be hit and can be removed.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced end-to-end against package src + apps/showcase. (1) Serialization: ran ExportAction::make('export')->format(CSV)->toArray(null,null,$resource) directly — output has NO `url` key (method POST, type bulk). Root cause: Action::toArray (Action.php:299-307) only fills url via resolveStockUrl when no ->url() and no callback; resolveStockUrl's match (Action.php:353-360) covers only row view/edit/delete/restore and ['bulk','delete'], with default => null, so ['bulk','export'] yields null. ExportAction (packages/export) declares no ->url() and no ->action() callback (overrides execute() directly; hasCallback() checks $this->action which is null). InertiaDataBuilder.php:209 serializes bulk actions via serializeMany(...,$resource) → Action::toArray($user,null,$resource), so $resource is correctly forwarded yet still produces no url. (2) Frontend: ArqelIndexPage.tsx:193 `action.url ?? `/arqel-dev/actions/${action.name}`` → POSTs to /arqel-dev/actions/export. (3) Routing: `php artisan route:list` in apps/showcase shows ZERO routes matching /actions/, bulk-actions, or arqel-dev — the only bulk route is core's `admin/{resource}/bulk/{action}` (arqel.resources.bulk), which expects `record_ids` (matches the frontend payload). The arqel-actions.php package routes (bulk-actions/{action}, expecting 'ids') are not present in the route table at all. So the export bulk button POSTs to a non-existent endpoint → 404, unreachable from the UI. Skeptic checks that did NOT refute it: confirmed actions package is symlinked into vendor; confirmed $resource has a slug ('posts') so it is not a missing-slug edge; confirmed deleteBulk works only because it hits the ['bulk','delete'] stock arm (URL /admin/posts/bulk/delete) AND core's controller special-cases action==='delete' (ResourceController.php:170-171) — proving the framework intends bulk actions to ride the core admin/{resource}/bulk/{action} route, which export is simply never pointed at. Classification = framework: the showcase used only documented first-party API (export package's ExportAction::make + core's ->bulkActions() slot) and did nothing wrong; both faulty layers (PHP serializer + JS fallback) live in framework packages, not the app. The candidate's only minor inaccuracy (discussing the bulk-actions/{action} 'ids' mismatch) is moot since the fallback URL never matches that route shape anyway — its core conclusion (404, export unreachable) holds. Severity high: a shipped, advertised bulk action is completely non-functional from the UI, but it is a feature-dead path rather than a crash/security/data-loss blocker.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: Action::toArray() (packages/actions/src/Action.php:294-307) only fills url for stock verbs via resolveStockUrl(), whose match (Action.php:353-360) has no arm for ['bulk','export'] -> returns null, so the bulk 'export' action ships with no `url`. Frontend ArqelIndexPage.tsx:193 `const url = action.url ?? `/arqel-dev/actions/${action.name}`` then POSTs to /arqel-dev/actions/export. The only bulk route registered in showcase is `admin/{resource}/bulk/{action}` (verified via `php artisan route:list | grep bulk`); the actions package route `{resource}/bulk-actions/{action}` (packages/actions/routes/arqel-actions.php:18) is not loaded, and even it expects input 'ids' while the frontend sends 'record_ids' (ArqelIndexPage.tsx:202). So the export bulk button targets a non-existent endpoint (404) with a mismatched payload. Bulk export is unreachable from the UI independent of bug #1.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Rich Column::toArray() emits 'alignment' but TS contract + DataTable read 'align' — column alignment silently ignored for all tabled resources
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In Column::toArray() rename the emitted key 'alignment' to 'align' (default to 'start' when null) to match the ColumnBase TS contract and DataTable's col?.align reads.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced structurally against package src. Column::toArray() (packages/table/src/Column.php:373) serializes 'alignment' => $this->alignment, but the published TS contract ColumnBase (packages-js/types/src/tables.ts:33) declares a REQUIRED 'align: ColumnAlign', and the React consumer DataTable.tsx reads col?.align at all four alignment sites (lines 155-156 header, 242-243 cell). The rich Table payload never emits 'align', so col?.align is always undefined and alignCenter()/alignEnd() have zero rendering effect. The parallel field-derived fallback path (InertiaDataBuilder.php:163) correctly emits 'align' => 'start', so the two index payload paths disagree and the primary Table path is the one violating the contract. Skeptic could not refute: DataTable has no 'alignment' fallback read; 'align' is required (not optional) in the TS type; and the existing unit test (ColumnTypesTest.php:44-47) only asserts the PHP getter getAlignment(), never the serialized toArray() key, which is why the regression slipped through. Classified framework-bug, not app-misuse: showcase (when aligning) uses the documented alignCenter()/alignEnd() helpers correctly; the defect is entirely in the framework serialization key, so the fix belongs in arqel-dev/table, not the app. Severity downgraded from high to medium: real and silently non-functional, but cosmetic (text alignment only), no data/security impact, and only manifests when a user opts into non-default alignment.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/table/src/Column.php:373 emits key 'alignment'; packages-js/types/src/tables.ts:33 ColumnBase declares required 'align: ColumnAlign'; packages-js/ui/src/table/DataTable.tsx:155-156,242-243 read col?.align (always undefined for rich path). Probe of PostResource index payload shows status/title columns serialized with 'alignment' and no 'align' key. The plain-fallback path (InertiaDataBuilder::deriveColumnsFromFields, packages/core/src/Support/InertiaDataBuilder.php:165) correctly emits 'align', so the two index payload paths disagree and the primary (Table) path violates the published type. alignCenter()/alignEnd() never affect the rendered table.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: SelectField with static options() emits no 'in:' validation rule — server accepts out-of-range choice values (validation gap / mass-assignment of invalid enum)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/fields/src/Types/SelectField.php:170 (resolveOptions present, but no getDefaultRules() override on the class — gap spans lines 31-200)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Add getDefaultRules() to SelectField deriving Rule::in(array_keys($resolvedOptions)) when options are statically resolvable and !creatable && !allowCustomValues; for multiple mode emit ['array'] plus 'name.*' => in:. Inherited by Radio/MultiSelect.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced concretely against package src + showcase. Direct probe: (new SelectField('status'))->options(['draft'=>...,'published'=>...,'archived'=>...]) yields getValidationRules()===[] and FieldRulesExtractor::extract gives status=>[]; Illuminate Validator::make(['status'=>'totally-not-valid'], rules)->passes()===true. No 'in:' constraint emitted on the security boundary. Skeptic checks performed: (1) Post model — 'status' is $fillable with NO enum cast and no DB enum, so the invalid value persists verbatim; no secondary guard catches it. (2) allowCustomValues() is only serialized to the JS prop, never consumed in PHP validation — and its existence as an opt-out proves the intended DEFAULT is to constrain to the option set. (3) Every other constrained field type ships getDefaultRules() (Number/Boolean/Date/Url/Color/Image/Slug/Email/File); SelectField/RadioField/MultiSelectField are the only constrained inputs that break this self-validating-field contract — an inconsistency, not design. Framework-vs-misuse: the showcase uses the exact canonical documented pattern (PLANNING/05-api-php.md:334+, SKILL.md, and SelectField's own docblock all show options([...]) with no manual rules(['in:...']) anywhere). If the showcase is wrong, every doc example is wrong too — so the fix belongs in the framework (SelectField), not the app. The ValidationBridge already supports in:->z.enum translation, so the rule was meant to flow through. Could not refute. Severity medium not blocker: real server-side validation gap reaching the DB, but exploitation needs a crafted/tampered request (rendered <select> constrains normal users); bounded to a free-text string column accepting arbitrary values — no auth/crypto/RCE impact.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/fields/src/Types/SelectField.php has no getDefaultRules() override (grep of getDefaultRules across packages/fields confirms Boolean/Date/Url/Number/Color/Image/Slug do, Select/Radio/MultiSelect do not). Probe: FieldRulesExtractor::extract(PostResource::effectiveFields()) yields status=>[] ; Validator::make(['status'=>'totally-not-a-valid-status', 'author_id'=>99999], rules)->passes() === true. Closed-option select on the security boundary (server) does not constrain to its own option set while the React layer does.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Plain-index column fallback emits a different column shape than the rich Table path (align vs alignment, tooltip/copyable present, togglable/hiddenByDefault/url/openUrlInNewTab absent)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In Column::toArray() rename key 'alignment' => 'align' (and add 'tooltip' => null) to match the canonical ColumnBase TS contract / DataTable; the plain fallback is already correct.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced against package src. Two paths build the `columns` payload for the same React `<DataTable>`: the plain fallback (`InertiaDataBuilder::deriveColumnsFromFields`, packages/core/src/Support/InertiaDataBuilder.php:154-167) and the rich Table path (`Column::toArray`, packages/table/src/Column.php:362-378). The candidate is confirmed as a divergence, but the detection agent inverted which side is wrong. The canonical contract is the TypeScript type `ColumnBase` (packages-js/types/src/tables.ts:24-39), which the consumer DataTable.tsx reads: it uses key `align` (lines 155-156, 242-243) and `tooltip`, and has NO `togglable`/`hiddenByDefault`/`openUrlInNewTab`. The PLAIN fallback emits exactly this shape (`align`, `tooltip`) and is correct. The RICH `Column::toArray()` emits `alignment` (line 373) — which DataTable never reads (no remap layer exists anywhere in packages-js/ui/src or the table package; verified by grep) — and omits `tooltip`. Concrete consequence: any Resource using `Table::make()` with `->alignCenter()`/`->alignEnd()` has its column alignment silently dropped on the index page, because `col.align` is undefined. The skeptic could not refute: no serializer/subclass renames `alignment`→`align`, and the TS type + component agree on `align`. Not app-misuse: showcase resources (PostResource/TicketResource/AuthorResource) use TextColumn/BadgeColumn per documented API; they simply don't exercise alignment, so the defect is latent in showcase but is a genuine framework contract mismatch. Severity medium (not blocker): main-path silent feature breakage but core rendering still works and showcase doesn't trigger it; the missing-`tooltip` and extra togglable/url keys are secondary (extra keys are harmless, tooltip is unused by DataTable today).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/core/src/Support/InertiaDataBuilder.php:154-167 deriveColumnsFromFields emits {align, tooltip, copyable, hidden, hiddenOnMobile, width} but NOT {togglable, hiddenByDefault, url, openUrlInNewTab}; packages/table/src/Column.php:362-378 toArray emits {alignment, togglable, hiddenByDefault, url, openUrlInNewTab} but NOT {tooltip}. A Resource that declares no Table (plain fallback) and one that declares a Table produce structurally divergent 'columns' entries for the same React <DataTable>, so component behavior depends on which path built the payload.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Sidebar never renders navigation icons; icon-collapse mode shows blank items
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In Sidebar.tsx render a lucide icon from item.icon (e.g. a DynamicIcon/icon-name lookup) inside <SidebarMenuButton> alongside the label, so collapsible="icon" mode has a glyph.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Confirmed end-to-end and could not be refuted. Server emits the icon: HandleArqelInertiaRequests.php:225 sets 'icon' => $resourceClass::getNavigationIcon(), and Resource.php:92 exposes the getter backed by static $navigationIcon. All four showcase resources correctly declare it (PostResource file-text:50, TicketResource life-buoy:42, SettingResource settings:38, AuthorResource user-pen:29). The hook surfaces it: NavigationItemPayload.icon at useNavigation.ts:14-18. But Sidebar.tsx:51-61 renders only {item.label} and never reads item.icon — grep across packages-js/ui/src/shell confirms NO shell component references .icon. NOT app-misuse: showcase uses the documented Resource API correctly; the server serializes correctly; the defect is purely the UI component ignoring a field it receives. Refutation attempts failed: (a) the framework's own doc comment HandleArqelInertiaRequests.php:200-202 states the Sidebar renders 'menu items with icons', so omission is a bug not intent; (b) icon rendering is feasible — lucide-react is a direct dependency of packages-js/ui (package.json) and used elsewhere, and shadcn SidebarMenuButton with collapsible='icon' (Sidebar.tsx:39) is built to show an icon when collapsed. Severity calibrated to medium rather than high: expanded sidebar still shows labels (navigation works), but the collapsed icon-rail (the chosen collapse mode) renders blank items with no glyph — degraded UX, not a crash or data loss.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages-js/ui/src/shell/Sidebar.tsx:51-61 renders only {item.label} and never reads item.icon, despite useNavigation()/NavigationItemPayload (packages-js/hooks/src/useNavigation.ts:14) exposing icon and the server emitting it (HandleArqelInertiaRequests.php:225). All showcase resources declare navigationIcon (PostResource file-text, TicketResource life-buoy, SettingResource settings, AuthorResource user-pen) yet none appear. With (Sidebar.tsx:39) the collapsed rail has no icon to show, leaving items blank.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Navigation has no group-level sort; group order is an accidental side effect of item sort interleaving
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In buildNavigation(), order groups by the index of each item's group in $panel->getNavigationGroups() (groups not listed fall back to min item-sort), then by item sort within a group — instead of the flat per-item usort.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced against package src. buildNavigation() (HandleArqelInertiaRequests.php:206-237) emits a FLAT item list and usort()s by per-item 'sort' only (line 234). 'group' is a bare string with no group-level sort. The client groupItems() (Sidebar.tsx:74-85) builds groups in first-encounter order of that flat list, so group order is determined entirely by each group's minimum item sort plus PHP usort stability for ties. This is NOT app-misuse: (1) showcase uses the documented per-resource $navigationGroup/$navigationSort API exactly as api-php.md:573-581 prescribes; (2) the framework itself DOCUMENTS Panel::navigationGroups(['Content','Access Control']) at api-php.md:551-554 as the explicit mechanism to ORDER groups, and Panel stores/exposes it (Panel.php:248-250, getNavigationGroups() at 327-330) — but grep proves getNavigationGroups() has ZERO consumers in the entire payload path or the JS client. The documented group-ordering API is dead: a developer setting navigationGroups([...]) gets it silently ignored. Skeptic checks performed: searched every consumer of getNavigationGroups/navigationGroups across packages/core/src and packages-js/ui (none); confirmed no re-sort by group order before reaching Inertia; confirmed nav package's NavigationGroup has a real sort() concept that core's auto-nav builder bypasses entirely. Could not refute.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/core/src/Http/Middleware/HandleArqelInertiaRequests.php:206-236 buildNavigation() emits a flat item list and usort()s by per-item navigationSort only (line 234); group is just a string field with no group sort. Client groupItems() (packages-js/ui/src/shell/Sidebar.tsx:74-85) builds groups in first-encounter order of that flat list. Verified showcase flat order = Posts(Content,10), Tickets(Support,10), Settings(System,10), Authors(Content,20): Authors is split from Posts by two other groups, and group order Content>Support>System is determined solely by each group's minimum item sort, not by any independent group ordering. Equal item sorts across groups make order rely on PHP usort stability.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: vendor:publish of migrations fails — providers register migration via hasMigration() (expects .php.stub) but packages ship plain .php
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Rename shipped migrations to the un-timestamped name Spatie expects (database/migrations/create_arqel_versions_table.php[.stub] and create_arqel_state_transitions_table.php[.stub]); Spatie generates the timestamp at publish time.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced against Spatie laravel-package-tools src. In ProcessMigrations::bootPackageMigrations (vendor/.../ProcessMigrations.php:22-28), Spatie resolves the source as database/migrations/{name}.php where {name} is the exact string passed to hasMigration() — here 'create_arqel_versions_table' (NO timestamp). If that .php is absent it appends '.stub' as a fallback (line 26-28). The shipped file is '2026_05_01_000000_create_arqel_versions_table.php' (WITH timestamp). I emulated the resolver in PHP: both candidate source paths (create_arqel_versions_table.php and .php.stub) are MISSING; the real file is only found under the timestamped name Spatie never looks for. Under vendor:publish, the final $from handed to Laravel's VendorPublishCommand::publishItem is the non-existent .php.stub fallback, and line 247 emits exactly 'Can't locate path: <...>' — matching the reported error. Same defect in workflow (WorkflowServiceProvider.php:39 + 2026_05_01_000000_create_arqel_state_transitions_table.php). The detection agent's wording ('expects .php.stub') is imprecise — .stub is only a fallback; the true root cause is the timestamp-prefix mismatch — but its conclusion (publish broken) is correct. This is a FRAMEWORK (package) defect, not app-misuse: apps/showcase never exercises this path — it ships its own hand-written copies (2026_06_05_100010/100011_create_arqel_versions/state_transitions_table.php) instead of using vendor:publish, so the showcase neither triggers nor masks a correct package. Severity medium not blocker: runsMigrations defaults to false (HasMigrations.php:7), so no runtime/schema crash and loadMigrationsFrom isn't reached — only the public vendor:publish --tag=arqel-{versioning,workflow}-migrations path is broken for downstream consumers, with manual authoring as a workaround.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/versioning/src/VersioningServiceProvider.php:33 hasMigration(create_arqel_versions_table); but the shipped file is .php not .php.stub. Same for packages/workflow/src/WorkflowServiceProvider.php:39. vendor:publish --tag=arqel-versioning-migrations errors: cannot locate path create_arqel_versions_table.php.stub

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Bulk delete (deleteBulk) fires without a confirm dialog while per-row delete requires confirmation
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Render bulk actions through <ActionButton> (like ActionMenu/row actions) so action.requiresConfirmation opens ConfirmDialog before router.visit, instead of a raw <button> firing the destructive visit immediately on click.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced against package src, not just the E2E test. (1) Framework intent: Actions::deleteBulk() (packages/actions/src/Actions.php:63-75) explicitly calls ->requiresConfirmation()->modalHeading('Delete selected records?'), identical to delete() — so the stock bulk-delete is DECLARED to confirm. (2) PHP serializes it: Action::toArray() (Action.php:320-321) emits requiresConfirmation + confirmation; the JS ActionSchema/ActionsBundle types (packages-js/types/src/actions.ts:69-71,88) carry these on bulk actions. (3) The shared ActionButton (ActionButton.tsx:63-72,100-108) correctly gates on action.requiresConfirmation and opens ConfirmDialog. Row actions reach it via ActionMenu inline path (≤3 actions), so per-row delete confirms. (4) The DEFECT: ArqelIndexPage.tsx:184-213 renders bulk actions as a raw <button> with an inline onClick that calls router.visit immediately, never touching ActionButton and never reading requiresConfirmation/confirmation. So a destructive mass-delete fires with no confirm, directly contradicting the framework's own factory. Skeptic checks for app-misuse FAILED to refute: the showcase used the documented stock Actions::deleteBulk() factory (PostResource.php:148-149, plus Setting/Ticket/Author resources) and added nothing — the confirmation it relies on is the framework's own promise. The fix belongs in framework JS, not the app. (Related latent issue: ActionMenu dropdown path lines 72-88 also drops confirmation via onSelect→onInvoke, but PostResource's ≤3 row actions use the inline ActionButton path, so per-row delete is unaffected.) Not intended design: nothing documents bulk delete as confirmation-exempt, and the factory proves the opposite.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: apps/showcase E2E tests/e2e/01-resources.spec.ts: per-row delete opens a role=dialog confirm; bulk Delete-selected button fires immediately via router.visit with no confirm. Inconsistent destructive-action UX.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Login and register GET routes lack 'guest' middleware; authenticated users see the login/register form instead of being redirected
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Add 'guest' to the middleware array of the GET (and POST attempt) login/register routes, mirroring the password-reset routes at :161-175.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Confirmed against source. Routes.php:60-62 (GET login) and :94-96 (GET register) register only ['web', HandleArqelInertiaRequests::class] with no 'guest' middleware, whereas the password-reset routes at :161-175 — written by the same author in the same file — consistently apply 'guest'. This proves the omission is an inconsistency, not a deliberate global no-guest policy. LoginController::showForm() and RegisterController::showForm() render the Inertia page unconditionally with no auth-state check, so an already-authenticated user hitting /admin/login re-renders the login form instead of being redirected. Classification = framework bug, NOT app-misuse: the showcase (ArqelServiceProvider:52-70) uses the documented ->login() API and the routes are self-registered by the auth package with their own explicit middleware arrays; the panel-wide 'auth' middleware applies to resource routes, not these auth routes. Skeptic refutation attempts all failed: (1) controller does not guard auth state; (2) no panel middleware covers these self-registered routes; (3) no custom override of the 'guest' alias in the showcase, so Laravel's default RedirectIfAuthenticated would correctly redirect; (4) the consistent 'guest' on password-reset routes refutes 'intended design'. Severity low: purely a UX/correctness inconsistency (authenticated user sees a form instead of a redirect); no security/data exposure or privilege escalation, though standard scaffolds (Breeze/Fortify) do apply 'guest' here.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/auth/src/Routes.php:60-62 (GET login) and :94-95 (GET register) register only ['web', HandleArqelInertiaRequests::class] with no 'guest' middleware, while forgot/reset routes at :161-175 correctly include 'guest'. Panel-wide middleware ['web','auth','arqel.tenant:optional'] applies to resource routes, not these self-registered auth routes, so an already-authenticated user hitting /admin/login re-renders the Inertia login form (LoginController::showForm) rather than redirecting to /admin.

notBugs.title: TableWidget with no explicit columns relies on a duck-typed default-column resolver that may render empty
notBugs.reasoning: The candidate's core premise is false: there is NO "default-column resolver" / "default column derivation" anywhere. PHP TableWidget::serialiseColumns() (packages/widgets/src/TableWidget.php:123-136) only iterates the explicit $this->columns array (default []); it never derives columns from records. JS TableCard.tsx only maps widget.columns. grep confirmed no array_keys / Object.keys / derive logic on either side. So the reported failure mode ("if the resolver regresses, renders empty") cannot occur — there is no resolver to regress. The actual deterministic behavior: when columns() is omitted (as in showcase recent_posts, which sets query/limit/seeAllUrl only), data() emits columns: [] with records populated, and TableCard renders a table with an empty header and N body rows each with zero cells (a structurally empty table). This is the DOCUMENTED, tested contract — the class docblock and SKILL.md state columns are duck-typed and explicit-only ("others are dropped silently"); TableWidgetTest asserts columns === [] when none are set. No documented rule requires auto-derivation, and none requires the app to call columns(), so the showcase isn't violating a contract — it uses the public typed API as-is. Nothing crashes, no exception, no data loss; payload is correct per contract and the React side honors it faithfully. At most this is a minor framework DX/usability gap (an omitted-columns + non-empty-records table could fall back to record keys or warn), not a functional framework defect. Skeptic refutation holds: the alleged regression path is fictional and the observed behavior is the spec. isReal=false.
